items.headline: Timothée Chalamet's CNN Slip
items.detail: Said 'no one cares' about ballet and opera on CNN, alienating Academy voters and likely costing him the Best Actor win despite Golden Globe and Critics Choice victories.

items.headline: Josh Safdie On-Set Controversy
items.detail: The Marty Supreme director faced allegations of placing a minor in an uncomfortable on-set situation with a non-professional actor, causing major damage to his Oscar campaign.

items.headline: Wicked: For Good — Total Shutout
items.detail: The first Wicked scored 10 nominations. The sequel got zero — not a single nod for Cynthia Erivo, Ariana Grande, or director Jon M. Chu. Michelle Yeoh publicly called it 'truly, truly disappointing.'

items.headline: Paul Mescal Snubbed Again
items.detail: Despite near-universal praise for his performance in Hamnet, Mescal was passed over for Best Actor while co-star Jessie Buckley got a nomination. His second high-profile snub in two years.

items.headline: Sinners: Most Nominations Ever, But Underdog for Best Picture
items.detail: With a record-breaking 16 nominations and a 97% RT score, Sinners made horror history — yet sits at just 27% odds for Best Picture against One Battle After Another's 69%. The Academy has never been here before.

items.headline: Kate Hudson's Shock Comeback Nomination
items.detail: Absent from the Oscar conversation for over 20 years, Hudson's surprise nomination left even her fans stunned. Whether it's a feel-good story or an eyebrow-raising pick depends who you ask.

items.headline: Conan O'Brien Hosting
items.detail: The beloved late-night host returns to a major awards stage for the first time in years. Known for his quick wit and self-deprecating humor, expectations are sky-high for the ceremony.
